occurrence_sentence: Fresh off its latest $5.5 million funding round this month that was led by Quantonation and The Group Ventures, evolutionQ plans to advance product development, expand service offerings and grow sales across Europe and North America, focussing on its flagship product BasejumpQDNTM.

Fresh off its [latest $5.5 million funding round](https://thequantuminsider.com/2022/06/13/evolutionq-secures-5-5-million-us-in-series-a-funding/) this month that was led by Quantonation and The Group Ventures, [evolutionQ](https://evolutionq.com/) plans to advance product development, expand service offerings and grow sales across Europe and North America, focussing on its flagship product BasejumpQDNTM.

Its latest funding, a [€16.1-million ($16.9M) Seed Round](https://thequantuminsider.com/2022/06/10/qubit-pharmaceuticals-raises-e16-1-million-in-seed-round/) finalized in early June and led by [Quantonation](https://thequantuminsider.com/2022/06/08/quantum-investors/), Omnes, XAnge, and Octave Klaba, will be used to enrich its portfolio of drug candidates, as the company has already launched three research programs on Covid-19, cancer and inflammation, and wants to launch ten, each representing an investment of $1.5 to $4 million over three years, according to Robert Marino, Qubit Pharmaceutical's CEO in an interview with France Detail Zero.

interaction_phrase: Established in 2020 by the five-person founding team of Jay Ponder
occurrence_sentence: Established in 2020 by the five-person founding team of Jay Ponder, Pengyu Ren, J.-P. Piquemal, Louis Largardère, and Matthieu Montes, [Qubit Pharmaceuticals](https://qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/) is a quantum computing startup that is developing a platform to accelerate drug discovery by way of ATLAS — built upon over 30 years of research — a software suite for the discovery and test of drug candidates running on supercomputers to co-design new drugs with pharma and biotech companies.

Its latest funding, a [€16.1-million ($16.9M) Seed Round](https://thequantuminsider.com/2022/06/10/qubit-pharmaceuticals-raises-e16-1-million-in-seed-round/) finalized in early June and led by [Quantonation](https://thequantuminsider.com/2022/06/08/quantum-investors/), Omnes, XAnge, and Octave Klaba, will be used to enrich its portfolio of drug candidates, as the company has already launched three research programs on Covid-19, cancer and inflammation, and wants to launch ten, each representing an investment of $1.5 to $4 million over three years, according to Robert Marino, Qubit Pharmaceutical's CEO in an interview with France Detail Zero.

occurrence_sentence: Established in 2020 by the five-person founding team of Jay Ponder, Pengyu Ren, J.-P. Piquemal, Louis Largardère, and Matthieu Montes, [Qubit Pharmaceuticals](https://qubit-pharmaceuticals.com/) is a quantum computing startup that is developing a platform to accelerate drug discovery by way of ATLAS — built upon over 30 years of research — a software suite for the discovery and test of drug candidates running on supercomputers to co-design new drugs with pharma and biotech companies.
